segments.text: You see this cat everywhere in Japan,
but it was born out of a child's crayon
drawing. It's so famous, Japanese people
don't say delivery anymore. They just
say Kuroniko, a black cat. But why?
1957, a designer was failing at the most
important job of his life. Teeshi
Shimizu had to create the Japanese
delivery logo and he was stuck. Days
passed, nothing worked and the company
needed their brand identity. When his
daughter came home from school, she had
drawn a mama cat carrying her baby. He
looked at it and thought that's it. A
child's crayon drawing became a
corporate logo. But then something
magical happened. It became the most
trusted logo in Japan. Companies pay
millions of dollar just to have this cat
on their packages. Because when you
handle packages like family, the whole
country trust you with theirs. That's
how you build a brand that lasts for
more than 70 years. For more amazing
design stories, subscribe.
